codeReview.summary: Core directory loading and subsequence matching are implemented with good test coverage, but the final fuzzy score formula diverges from the referenced Ruby algorithm by excluding base_score from density/length multipliers.

issues.description: The implementation applies density/length multipliers only to the fuzzy component (`score - base_score`) and then adds `base_score` back (`score = fuzzy_component * density * length_factor + entry.base_score`). In the reference algorithm (`/Users/js/code/3rd/try/lib/fuzzy.rb`, lines 129-132), both multipliers are applied to the total running score (which already includes base_score). This changes ranking behavior for non-empty queries and does not match the specified 'exact algorithm' reference.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The rust-worker skill requires running spec tests when corresponding specs exist, but this run reported `followedProcedure: true` without running spec-test commands.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Skill file `.factory/skills/rust-worker/SKILL.md` step 7 requires `cargo build && bash spec/tests/runner.sh ./target/debug/lab` when relevant specs exist. Handoff `verification.commandsRun` for session `0519e5a9-d098-4c99-a8cc-7b9305636999` includes cargo test/clippy/check only; no spec runner command is present.

summary: Review failed due to a blocking scoring-formula mismatch in `src/fuzzy.rs` versus the referenced Ruby implementation. Noted one shared-state skill/procedure consistency gap around spec-test execution reporting.
